hinge: An isolated enamel mass in a furcation is an enamel pearl — ectopic enamel formation. It differs from dens invaginatus and from generalized enamel hypoplasia patterns.
Oral hygiene should begin with eruption of the first primary teeth (usually primary incisors). Waiting for molars or permanent first molars delays plaque control and caries prevention.

Large restorations complicate orthodontic treatment because bond strength to restorative surfaces is less predictable and restorations or brackets often fracture or fail during debonding and appliance removal. The restoration may also complicate bonding and rebonding throughout treatment.

An implant placed while residual jaw growth continues becomes relatively displaced as adjacent alveolus and teeth erupt with osseous growth. Maxillary central incisor drifting frontosuperiorly years later reflects continued growth around a fixed implant placed before growth completion.

Deflection on closure after a new onlay covering a cusp means the restoration lacks a stable centric stop. Without a centric contact the mandible shifts until another tooth contacts, causing the deflection.

Addition silicone (PVS) is the preferred final crown impression material due to high accuracy, excellent dimensional stability, and fine surface detail for dies and margins. Alginate lacks the precision and stability for definitive fixed prosthodontics.

Angle's molar classification is based on the mesiodistal relationship of the permanent first molars — specifically the maxillary first molar mesiobuccal cusp relative to the mandibular first molar buccal groove.
Miller tooth mobility Grade I is the first detectable horizontal mobility up to approximately 1 mm. Grade II exceeds 1 mm horizontally; Grade III includes vertical depression mobility.
Intrapulpal calcifications (pulp stones) obstruct canal access and are best refined and removed under magnification using ultrasonic tips rather than relying solely on bulk chemical agents.

Intraoral surgical flaps enter a field already colonized by oral flora, so they are classified as clean-contaminated wounds — not sterile clean or grossly dirty wounds by CDC surgical wound classification.

A broken distal cusp next to a Class I amalgam usually means the cavity preparation undermined enamel and dentin (wide isthmus, deep flaring, unsupported cuspal enamel), so the cusp flexed and fractured under normal function.

Calcium hydroxide's therapeutic effect rests on its high pH (strong alkalinity) which kills bacteria and stimulates hard-tissue formation in pulp capping, apexification, and related endodontic procedures.
Patients with active influenza are contagious. Elective dental treatment should be deferred until the patient is no longer infectious to protect staff and other patients from transmission.

Severe limited mouth opening with mandibular deviation in a child suggests TMJ ankylosis. Definitive management is surgical gap arthroplasty with postoperative physiotherapy, not NSAIDs or bite planes alone.

When negative reinforcement and parental behavior methods fail for persistent thumb-sucking, a fixed habit-breaking appliance (palatal crib) physically interrupts digit placement and is the standard dental next step beyond behavioral approaches.

Acid etching demineralizes enamel surface to open micropores for resin tag formation, creating micromechanical retention and a continuous enamel-composite seal that reduces microleakage at restoration margins.

Caries risk is assessed before periodontal therapy because scaling and surgery often expose root surfaces. High-risk patients then develop root caries and marked dentin hypersensitivity on newly uncovered cervical root surfaces.
A young child with mouth sores, low-grade fever, and increased drooling is classic for primary herpetic gingivostomatitis. Herpangina mainly affects the posterior soft palate and pharynx.
Bence Jones proteins are immunoglobulin free light chains detected in urine and are a classic laboratory finding associated with multiple myeloma.
Pediatric ibuprofen is dosed on a weight-based schedule every 6-8 hours, not an every-4-hour adult interval. The every-6-hour dosing is the standard pediatrics approach.
The O'Leary plaque control record scores plaque presence after staining with a disclosing agent. A disclosing agent is required to visualize and score all tooth surfaces.
Established plaque-associated gingival inflammation is dominated by gram-negative anaerobic bacterial shifts, not pure staphylococcal or simple gram-positive flora alone.

After completing a course of antibiotics for endocarditis-related indication, delaying same-class dental antibiotic prophylaxis approximately 10 days allows resistant oral flora to recover before re-challenge with the same antibiotic class.
Small yellowish-white buccal mucosal nodules are Fordyce spots — ectopic sebaceous glands. These are normal anatomical variants, not serous salivary gland pathology.
When heavy calculus prevents accurate probing, gross scaling and debridement must be performed first so the probe can reach the true base of the pocket and pocket depths can be measured reliably.

A fractured cusp on a vital tooth requires cuspal coverage to protect the remaining structure. A ceramic onlay restores the missing cusp while conserving more tooth than a full-coverage crown. Direct composite alone does not protect a missing cusp under functional load.

In a well-controlled diabetic/hypothyroid patient who loses consciousness during routine dental care, vasovagal syncope is by far the most common cause. Myxedema coma is rare and unlikely if hypothyroidism is well controlled.
Sudden dizziness and loss of consciousness in an otherwise healthy patient during routine dentistry is classic vasovagal syncope — the most frequent dental-office medical emergency.
Periodontitis grade is calculated using % bone loss divided by patient age. Thirty percent bone loss at age 35 equals approximately 0.86, which falls in Grade B (range 0.25-1.0).

Among the listed choices, only Resilon is an obturation-related root-filling material. However, clinically, true resin allergy contraindicates resin-based obturation systems and favors gutta-percha with a non-resin sealer — which is not offered among the options.
